tables.name: entries
tables.label: Entries
tables.description: Journal entries. Each entry has a date, body, mood, and may reference people, events, and tags — and can hold multiple independent parts.

tables.name: entry_parts
tables.label: Entry parts
tables.description: Independent parts of a single entry (text, rich text, a measurement, a photo) — kept in order under their entry, not separate entries.

tables.name: places
tables.label: Places
tables.description: Geolocations referenced in journal entries (name + coordinates).

tables.name: measurements
tables.label: Measurements
tables.description: A number logged over time (energy, sleep, weight, …). The Trends screen fits a trend and forecasts future values.

tables.name: connections
tables.label: Connections
tables.description: How people relate to each other — the edges of the People web (from, to, relationship).

tables.name: feelings
tables.label: Feelings
tables.description: Named scores you log whenever you like: sad 3.5/5, bored 1/5, motivated 10/10. Each can link to an entry — decode a day’s overall emotion into its parts.

tables.name: prefs
tables.label: Preferences
tables.description: Your defaults for Neujour — e.g. which day the calendar week starts on. A single row you tweak; not journal data.

tables.name: days
tables.label: Days
tables.description: One row per day — an overall summary + mood for the whole day, separate from the individual moments. Entries stay their own rows; a day just gathers them.
